body.title: Igor NE: structural polling tick replaces unbounded spin
body.result: COA._ne_tick_should_yield: 30s hard floor via IGOR_NE_TICK_INTERVAL; breaks NARRATIVE_GAP re-fire loop; 6 tests
body.intention: I intend that Igor's NE loop has a natural yield/check rhythm derived from actual TWM activation propagation speed, making 'check and yield' the structural default rather than something cognition must choose.
body.description:
Igor's NE loop currently spins unboundedly — when no action resolves, it generates meta-questions (NARRATIVE_GAP) rather than yielding. The fix is a structural polling interval: measure how fast TWM salience updates cycle at idle, use that as the natural tick size. Each tick: check for new observations worth acting on, act or yield. No unbounded spin.

Related to T-igor-ne-standing-goal-stall (behavioral fix); this is the structural complement — gives the loop a heartbeat regardless of what cognition chooses.

**Affected files:** TBD — devices/igor/cognition/ NE loop; TWM observation cycle

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** In scope: add tick/yield point to NE loop; derive interval from measured idle activation speed. Out of scope: changing NARRATIVE_GAP logic (that's T-igor-ne-standing-goal-stall), changing inference provider.

**Completion criteria:** Igor NE loop has a measurable tick interval; idle periods show periodic yield points in cognition.log rather than continuous spin; NARRATIVE_GAP loop cannot run more than N ticks without yielding (N defined by the measured interval).
